name: My Mother's Meatballs
description: From My Italian Kitchen (Menafe), p.156. NEEDS SCAN — title placeholder; ingredients and method to be captured from the book page before cooking.
author: Menafe

ai.cliffNotes: Bread soaked in milk is mixed into ground beef to create tender, juicy meatballs that stay moist through a combination of high-heat searing and low-heat braising with frequent basting.
keyTips: Soak bread until fully soggy and squeeze out the milk before mixing—this adds moisture and tenderness without making the mixture wet or dense
Get the oil very hot before adding meatballs and leave them undisturbed for 3 minutes to develop a proper crust that seals in juices
Baste frequently during the final 8-10 minutes of covered cooking to keep the exterior moist and prevent drying out
Form into 8 large patties rather than small balls—flattened rounds cook more evenly and have better surface area for browning
